title: ARCHER AVIATION Q1 2026 Earnings Release
text: Archer Aviation released Q1 2026 earnings with stock rising following announcement. Company reported revenue $1.6 million, net loss $217.7 million, and R&D spending $171.7 million as company advanced FAA Type Certification to Phase 4. Key metrics beat expectations: EPS -$0.21 versus forecast -$0.30, revenue $1.6M versus expected $918K. Adjusted EBITDA loss $172.5 million within guidance range $160M-$180M. Operational highlights include first eVTOL company to close Phase 3 of FAA four-phase type certification, expanded piloted flight testing, and takeover of Hawthorne Airport operations in Los Angeles. Infrastructure and commercial plans discussion included focusing domestically on EIPP markets in New York, Florida, California, working with Port Authority and private airports. Strong liquidity position approximately $1.8 billion supports ongoing development and certification activities.
